topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
statement: A European patent specification is published in the language of the proceedings and must include a translation of the claims into the other two official languages of the EPO.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Specifications of European patents shall be published in the language of the proceedings and shall include a translation of the claims in the other two official languages of the European Patent Office.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
statement: A European patent specification is published in the language of the proceedings and must include a translation of the whole specification into the other two official languages of the EPO.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Specifications of European patents shall be published in the language of the proceedings and shall include a translation of the claims in the other two official languages of the European Patent Office.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
scenario: Greenfield Bioscience S.A. has its principal place of business in Lisbon, Portugal, a Contracting State whose official language (Portuguese) is not an EPO official language. During examination of its European patent application, on the last day of a time limit Greenfield files a substantive submission (a document forming part of the proceedings but not part of the application as filed) in Portuguese. Greenfield then fails to file any translation of that submission into English, French or German within the prescribed period.
statement: Because Greenfield did not file the required translation of its Portuguese submission in due time, that document is deemed not to have been filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If any document, other than those documents making up the European patent application, is not filed in the prescribed language, or if any required translation is not filed in due time, the document shall be deemed not to have been filed.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
scenario: Greenfield Bioscience S.A. has its principal place of business in Lisbon, Portugal, a Contracting State whose official language (Portuguese) is not an EPO official language. During examination of its European patent application, on the last day of a time limit Greenfield files a substantive submission (a document forming part of the proceedings but not part of the application as filed) in Portuguese. Greenfield then fails to file any translation of that submission into English, French or German within the prescribed period.
statement: Because Greenfield did not file the required translation of its Portuguese submission in due time, that document is deemed to have been withdrawn.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: If any document, other than those documents making up the European patent application, is not filed in the prescribed language, or if any required translation is not filed in due time, the document shall be deemed not to have been filed.

statement: When a European patent application replaces the description with a reference to a previously filed application, a certified copy of that earlier application must be filed within two months of the filing of the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: a certified copy of the previously filed application shall be filed within two months of filing the application.

statement: When a European patent application replaces the description with a reference to a previously filed application, a certified copy of that earlier application must be filed within four months of the filing of the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: a certified copy of the previously filed application shall be filed within two months of filing the application.

scenario: On 14 March 2024, Helios Optics Ltd files documents at the EPO indicating that a European patent is sought, giving its name and address, and instead of a description, including a reference to a previously filed application stating that application's filing date, its file number, the office with which it was filed, and stating that the reference replaces the description and any drawings. A date of filing is accorded. Helios must now supply a certified copy of the previously filed application.
statement: Helios must file a certified copy of the previously filed application within two months of filing its European patent application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: a certified copy of the previously filed application shall be filed within two months of filing the application.

scenario: On 14 March 2024, Helios Optics Ltd files documents at the EPO indicating that a European patent is sought, giving its name and address, and instead of a description, including a reference to a previously filed application stating that application's filing date, its file number, the office with which it was filed, and stating that the reference replaces the description and any drawings. A date of filing is accorded. Helios must now supply a certified copy of the previously filed application.
statement: Helios must file a certified copy of the previously filed application within four months of filing its European patent application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: a certified copy of the previously filed application shall be filed within two months of filing the application.

topic: Article 121 – Further processing of the European patent application
statement: A request for further processing of a European patent application is made by paying the prescribed fee within two months of the communication concerning the failure to observe a time limit or the loss of rights, and the omitted act must be completed within that same period.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Further processing under Article 121, paragraph 1, shall be requested by payment of the prescribed fee within two months of the communication concerning either the failure to observe a time limit or a loss of rights. The omitted act shall be completed within the period for making the request.

topic: Article 121 – Further processing of the European patent application
statement: A request for further processing of a European patent application is made by paying the prescribed fee within four months of the communication concerning the failure to observe a time limit or the loss of rights, and the omitted act must be completed within that same period.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: within two months of the communication concerning either the failure to observe a time limit or a loss of rights

topic: Article 121 – Further processing of the European patent application
scenario: Helios Optics GmbH received a communication under Rule 71(3) EPC on 3 March 2025, inviting it to pay the grant and publishing fee and file the translations of the claims within four months. Helios missed that period, and on 14 July 2025 the EPO issued a communication noting that the application was deemed to be withdrawn. Helios wishes to use further processing to revive the application by completing the omitted acts.
statement: Helios must request further processing by paying the prescribed fee within two months of the 14 July 2025 communication, and must complete the omitted acts within that same two-month period.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Further processing under Article 121, paragraph 1, shall be requested by payment of the prescribed fee within two months of the communication concerning either the failure to observe a time limit or a loss of rights. The omitted act shall be completed within the period for making the request.

topic: Article 121 – Further processing of the European patent application
scenario: Helios Optics GmbH received a communication under Rule 71(3) EPC on 3 March 2025, inviting it to pay the grant and publishing fee and file the translations of the claims within four months. Helios missed that period, and on 14 July 2025 the EPO issued a communication noting that the application was deemed to be withdrawn. Helios wishes to use further processing to revive the application by completing the omitted acts.
statement: Helios must request further processing by paying the prescribed fee within four months of the 14 July 2025 communication, and must complete the omitted acts within that same four-month period.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Further processing under Article 121, paragraph 1, shall be requested by payment of the prescribed fee within two months of the communication concerning either the failure to observe a time limit or a loss of rights. The omitted act shall be completed within the period for making the request.

statement: As a general rule, a request for re-establishment of rights must be filed within two months of the removal of the cause of non-compliance with the period, but at the latest within one year of expiry of the unobserved time limit.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Any request for re-establishment of rights under Article 122, paragraph 1, shall be filed in writing within two months of the removal of the cause of non-compliance with the period, but at the latest within one year of expiry of the unobserved time limit.

statement: As a general rule, a request for re-establishment of rights must be filed within two months of the removal of the cause of non-compliance with the period, but at the latest within six months of expiry of the unobserved time limit.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Any request for re-establishment of rights under Article 122, paragraph 1, shall be filed in writing within two months of the removal of the cause of non-compliance with the period, but at the latest within one year of expiry of the unobserved time limit.

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd filed a European patent application. The applicant missed the time limit for paying the renewal fee, and the missed time limit was one for which further processing under Article 121 EPC was available. Helios, having taken all due care, now wishes to request re-establishment of rights in respect of that period.
statement: Because further processing under Article 121 EPC was available for the missed period, re-establishment of rights is ruled out for that period.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Re-establishment of rights shall be ruled out in respect of any period for which further processing under Article 121 is available

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd filed a European patent application. The applicant missed the time limit for paying the renewal fee, and the missed time limit was one for which further processing under Article 121 EPC was available. Helios, having taken all due care, now wishes to request re-establishment of rights in respect of that period.
statement: Because appeal under Article 121 EPC was available for the missed period, re-establishment of rights is ruled out for that period.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Re-establishment of rights shall be ruled out in respect of any period for which further processing under Article 121 is available

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights
statement: Where a party regards the EPO's noting of a loss of rights as inaccurate, it may apply for a decision on the matter within two months of the communication.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: it may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights
statement: Where a party regards the EPO's noting of a loss of rights as inaccurate, it may apply for a decision on the matter within four months of the communication.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: it may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights
scenario: Helios Optics S.A. filed a European patent application. After Helios failed to pay a renewal fee in time, the EPO issued a communication under Rule 112(1) EPC noting that a loss of rights had occurred. Helios's representative believes the EPO's finding is mistaken, since the fee was in fact validly paid, and wishes to obtain a formal appealable decision on the matter.
statement: Helios may, within two months of the communication noting the loss of rights, apply to the EPO for a decision on the matter.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights
scenario: Helios Optics S.A. filed a European patent application. After Helios failed to pay a renewal fee in time, the EPO issued a communication under Rule 112(1) EPC noting that a loss of rights had occurred. Helios's representative believes the EPO's finding is mistaken, since the fee was in fact validly paid, and wishes to obtain a formal appealable decision on the matter.
statement: Helios may, within four months of the communication noting the loss of rights, apply to the EPO for a decision on the matter.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

statement: For the purpose of filing a divisional application, the earlier application is pending up to but not including the date on which the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An application is pending up to (but not on) the date that the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent

statement: For the purpose of filing a divisional application, the earlier application is pending up to and including the date on which the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: An application is pending up to (but not on) the date that the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent

scenario: Helix Genomics filed European patent application EP-A as the parent. Helix later decided to file a divisional application, EP-B. Helix wanted to know the latest moment at which EP-A could still be considered pending for the purpose of filing the divisional. The examining division had drawn up the grant decision, and the European Patent Bulletin was due to mention the grant of EP-A on 15 March. Helix's attorney filed the divisional EP-B on exactly 15 March, the very date the Bulletin mentioned the grant.
statement: Because EP-A is pending only up to but not on the date the Bulletin mentions the grant, the divisional EP-B filed on 15 March (the date of mention of grant) was not validly filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span:
The parent application must be pending when a divisional application is filed. Reference is made in this regard to the observations in G 1/09 and J 18/09 as to what constitutes a pending application. In the case of an application filed as a divisional application from an application that is itself a divisional application, it is sufficient that the latter is still pending on the second divisional application's date of receipt.
An application is pending up to (but not on) the date that the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent

scenario: Helix Genomics filed European patent application EP-A as the parent. Helix later decided to file a divisional application, EP-B. Helix wanted to know the latest moment at which EP-A could still be considered pending for the purpose of filing the divisional. The examining division had drawn up the grant decision, and the European Patent Bulletin was due to mention the grant of EP-A on 15 March. Helix's attorney filed the divisional EP-B on exactly 15 March, the very date the Bulletin mentioned the grant.
statement: Because EP-A is pending up to and including the date the Bulletin mentions the grant, the divisional EP-B filed on 15 March (the date of mention of grant) was validly filed.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span:
The parent application must be pending when a divisional application is filed. Reference is made in this regard to the observations in G 1/09 and J 18/09 as to what constitutes a pending application. In the case of an application filed as a divisional application from an application that is itself a divisional application, it is sufficient that the latter is still pending on the second divisional application's date of receipt.
An application is pending up to (but not on) the date that the European Patent Bulletin mentions the grant of the patent

topic: Rule 20 – Publication of the mention of the inventor
statement: A designated inventor will be mentioned in the published European patent application and the European patent specification unless they inform the EPO in writing that they have waived their right to be so mentioned.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The designated inventor shall be mentioned in the published European patent application and the European patent specification, unless they inform the European Patent Office in writing that they have waived their right to be thus mentioned.

topic: Rule 20 – Publication of the mention of the inventor
statement: A designated inventor will be mentioned in the published European patent application and the European patent specification unless they inform the EPO orally that they have waived their right to be so mentioned.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The designated inventor shall be mentioned in the published European patent application and the European patent specification, unless they inform the European Patent Office in writing that they have waived their right to be thus mentioned.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent runs for a term of 20 years counted from the date of filing of the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent runs for a term of 20 years counted from the priority date of the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: NovaChem AG filed European patent application EP 3 999 111 on 14 March 2010, claiming priority from an earlier German application filed on 20 May 2009. The European patent was duly granted on 8 June 2014. NovaChem wants to know, as a matter of the basic EPC term rule, on what basis the 20-year term of its European patent is calculated.
statement: The basic term of NovaChem's European patent is 20 years calculated from 14 March 2010, the date of filing of the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: NovaChem AG filed European patent application EP 3 999 111 on 14 March 2010, claiming priority from an earlier German application filed on 20 May 2009. The European patent was duly granted on 8 June 2014. NovaChem wants to know, as a matter of the basic EPC term rule, on what basis the 20-year term of its European patent is calculated.
statement: The basic term of NovaChem's European patent is 20 years calculated from 20 May 2009, the priority date of the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent has a term of 20 years calculated from the date of filing of the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent has a term of 20 years calculated from the date of priority of the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: Helix Therapeutics AG filed a European patent application on 14 March 2008, claiming priority from an earlier German national application filed on 20 September 2007. The European patent was duly granted on 5 June 2012. Helix now wishes to know when the basic 20-year term of the European patent expires.
statement: The basic term of Helix's European patent runs for 20 years calculated from the filing date of 14 March 2008, not from the priority date or the grant date.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: Helix Therapeutics AG filed a European patent application on 14 March 2008, claiming priority from an earlier German national application filed on 20 September 2007. The European patent was duly granted on 5 June 2012. Helix now wishes to know when the basic 20-year term of the European patent expires.
statement: The basic term of Helix's European patent runs for 20 years calculated from the priority date of 20 September 2007.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent has a term of 20 years counted from the date of filing of the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
statement: A European patent has a term of 20 years counted from the date of priority of the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: NovaPharm AG filed a European patent application on 15 March 2010, claiming an earlier priority date of 20 April 2009. The patent was eventually granted on 10 June 2014. NovaPharm wants to know the basic term of the European patent, absent any extension under national provisions.
statement: The basic term of NovaPharm's European patent runs for 20 years from the filing date of 15 March 2010.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

topic: Article 63 – Term of the European patent
scenario: NovaPharm AG filed a European patent application on 15 March 2010, claiming an earlier priority date of 20 April 2009. The patent was eventually granted on 10 June 2014. NovaPharm wants to know the basic term of the European patent, absent any extension under national provisions.
statement: The basic term of NovaPharm's European patent runs for 20 years from the priority date of 20 April 2009.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The term of the European patent shall be 20 years from the date of filing of the application.

scenario: Helix Robotics Ltd and Mara Volkov jointly develop a sensor and wish to file a single European patent application. They are unsure whether two parties may file one European patent application together, and whether two separate applicants may instead designate different Contracting States in the same application.
statement: Helix Robotics Ltd and Mara Volkov may file the European patent application as joint applicants, and the EPC also permits two or more applicants to file by designating different Contracting States.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: A European patent application may also be filed either by joint applicants or by two or more applicants designating different Contracting States.

scenario: Helix Robotics Ltd and Mara Volkov jointly develop a sensor and wish to file a single European patent application. They are unsure whether two parties may file one European patent application together, and whether two separate applicants may instead designate different Contracting States in the same application.
statement: Helix Robotics Ltd and Mara Volkov may file the European patent application as joint applicants, but the EPC requires that two or more applicants each designate different Contracting States.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: A European patent application may also be filed either by joint applicants or by two or more applicants designating different Contracting States.

scenario: AlphaTech GmbH holds European patent EP 3 000 000. BetaCorp Ltd, a competitor, files a notice of opposition within the nine-month period. After filing, AlphaTech GmbH itself decides it wishes to challenge the validity of certain claims of its own granted patent and attempts to file a notice of opposition against EP 3 000 000.
statement: AlphaTech GmbH may not validly oppose its own patent EP 3 000 000, because the proprietor is excluded from the 'any person' who is entitled to file an opposition.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: "Any person" does not include the patent proprietor

scenario: AlphaTech GmbH holds European patent EP 3 000 000. BetaCorp Ltd, a competitor, files a notice of opposition within the nine-month period. After filing, AlphaTech GmbH itself decides it wishes to challenge the validity of certain claims of its own granted patent and attempts to file a notice of opposition against EP 3 000 000.
statement: AlphaTech GmbH may validly oppose its own patent EP 3 000 000, because the proprietor is included within the 'any person' who is entitled to file an opposition.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: "Any person" does not include the patent proprietor

statement: A European patent application with more than fifteen claims incurs a claims fee for the sixteenth claim and each claim after it.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Any European patent application comprising more than fifteen claims shall, in respect of the sixteenth and each subsequent claim, incur payment of claims fees as laid down in the Rules relating to Fees.

statement: A European patent application with more than fifteen claims incurs a claims fee for the eleventh claim and each claim after it.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Any European patent application comprising more than fifteen claims shall, in respect of the sixteenth and each subsequent claim, incur payment of claims fees as laid down in the Rules relating to Fees.

scenario: Helios Optics GmbH files a European patent application on 5 May 2023 containing a first set of 22 claims. The applicant does not pay any claims fees at filing. The EPO subsequently issues a communication noting that the claims fees due in respect of the 16th and each subsequent claim have not been paid.
statement: Because Helios Optics did not pay the claims fees on time, it may still pay them within one month of the EPO's communication concerning the failure to observe the time limit.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: they may still be paid within one month of a communication concerning the failure to observe the time limit.

scenario: Helios Optics GmbH files a European patent application on 5 May 2023 containing a first set of 22 claims. The applicant does not pay any claims fees at filing. The EPO subsequently issues a communication noting that the claims fees due in respect of the 16th and each subsequent claim have not been paid.
statement: Because Helios Optics did not pay the claims fees on time, it may still pay them within two months of the EPO's communication concerning the failure to observe the time limit.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: they may still be paid within one month of a communication concerning the failure to observe the time limit.

statement: Where two European applications from the same applicant designate the same states and their claims have the same filing or priority date and relate to the same invention, the applicant must amend the claims, withdraw overlapping designations, or choose which application is to proceed to grant.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the applicant must amend one or more of the applications so that the subject-matter of their claims is no longer the same, withdraw overlapping designations or choose which one of those applications is to proceed to grant.

statement: Where two European applications from the same applicant designate the same states and their claims have the same filing or priority date and relate to the same invention, the applicant may amend the claims, withdraw overlapping designations, or choose which application is to proceed to grant.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the applicant must amend one or more of the applications so that the subject-matter of their claims is no longer the same, withdraw overlapping designations or choose which one of those applications is to proceed to grant.

scenario: Helixon Ltd files European application EP-A on 3 March 2021 and, on the same day, files a second European application EP-B, both designating the same contracting states. Independent claim 1 of EP-A and independent claim 1 of EP-B are directed to exactly the same chemical compound, and both applications share the same effective date. EP-B proceeds to grant first. The examining division then turns to EP-A, which Helixon has not amended in any way.
statement: Because EP-A still claims the same subject-matter as the already-granted EP-B held by the same applicant, EP-A may be refused under Art. 97(2) in conjunction with Art. 125.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Otherwise, once one of the applications has been granted, the others will be refused under Art. 97(2) in conjunction with Art. 125 (G 4/19).

scenario: Helixon Ltd files European application EP-A on 3 March 2021 and, on the same day, files a second European application EP-B, both designating the same contracting states. Independent claim 1 of EP-A and independent claim 1 of EP-B are directed to exactly the same chemical compound, and both applications share the same effective date. EP-B proceeds to grant first. The examining division then turns to EP-A, which Helixon has not amended in any way.
statement: Because EP-A still claims the same subject-matter as the already-granted EP-B held by the same applicant, EP-A may be refused under Art. 97(2) in conjunction with Art. 123.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Otherwise, once one of the applications has been granted, the others will be refused under Art. 97(2) in conjunction with Art. 125 (G 4/19).

statement: Under the EPC, the task of drawing up European search reports falls to the Search Divisions.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The Search Divisions shall be responsible for drawing up European search reports.

statement: Under the EPC, the task of drawing up European search reports falls to the Examining Divisions.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The Search Divisions shall be responsible for drawing up European search reports.

scenario: NovaTech GmbH files a European patent application directed to a medical implant that combines a polymer coating (materials chemistry) with an embedded microsensor circuit (electronics). The EPO determines that the 'person skilled in the art' for this subject-matter must be regarded as more than one person, so the Search Division is enlarged to include one examiner specialised in materials chemistry and one in electronics. Both members carry out searches in their respective fields, and all documents they find are gathered together.
statement: All the documents found in the different technical fields by the various members of the enlarged Search Division are included in the same search report, and only a single member draws up the search opinion, consulting the other members where necessary.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: When the search division is enlarged this way, all the documents found in the various technical fields by its different members are included in the same search report. However, only one member draws up the search opinion, albeit in consultation with the members with expertise in the other technical fields where necessary.

scenario: NovaTech GmbH files a European patent application directed to a medical implant that combines a polymer coating (materials chemistry) with an embedded microsensor circuit (electronics). The EPO determines that the 'person skilled in the art' for this subject-matter must be regarded as more than one person, so the Search Division is enlarged to include one examiner specialised in materials chemistry and one in electronics. Both members carry out searches in their respective fields, and all documents they find are gathered together.
statement: All the documents found in the different technical fields by the various members of the enlarged Search Division are included in the same search report, and each member draws up a separate search opinion for their own technical field.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: only one member draws up the search opinion

statement: An Examining Division is composed of three technically qualified examiners, and where the nature of the decision so requires it is enlarged by the addition of a legally qualified examiner.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An Examining Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners. However, before a decision is taken on a European patent application, its examination shall, as a general rule, be entrusted to one member of the Examining Division. Oral proceedings shall be before the Examining Division itself. If the Examining Division considers that the nature of the decision so requires, it shall be enlarged by the addition of a legally qualified examiner.

statement: An Examining Division is composed of two technically qualified examiners, and where the nature of the decision so requires it is enlarged by the addition of a legally qualified examiner.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: An Examining Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners.

scenario: During the examination of European patent application EP 9 876 543, filed by NovaChem AG, the Examining Division concludes that the nature of the decision to be taken requires the addition of a legally qualified examiner. Once enlarged, the Division votes on the matter, and the vote ends in a tie.
statement: In the event of a tie in the vote, the vote of the Chairman of the Examining Division is decisive.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: In the event of parity of votes, the vote of the Chairman of the Examining Division shall be decisive.

scenario: During the examination of European patent application EP 9 876 543, filed by NovaChem AG, the Examining Division concludes that the nature of the decision to be taken requires the addition of a legally qualified examiner. Once enlarged, the Division votes on the matter, and the vote ends in a tie.
statement: In the event of a tie in the vote, the vote of the legally qualified examiner is decisive.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: In the event of parity of votes, the vote of the Chairman of the Examining Division shall be decisive.

statement: An Opposition Division is composed of three technically qualified examiners, at least two of whom must not have participated in the grant proceedings of the patent against which the opposition is directed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An Opposition Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners, at least two of whom shall not have taken part in the proceedings for grant of the patent to which the opposition relates.

statement: An Opposition Division is composed of three technically qualified examiners, at least one of whom must not have participated in the grant proceedings of the patent against which the opposition is directed.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: An Opposition Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners, at least two of whom shall not have taken part in the proceedings for grant of the patent to which the opposition relates.

scenario: Helix Biotech holds European patent EP 3 000 111. An opposition is filed by a competitor. The EPO assigns an Opposition Division composed of three technically qualified examiners, Dr. Adler, Dr. Brandt and Dr. Conti. Dr. Adler had taken part in the examination proceedings that led to grant of the patent; Dr. Brandt and Dr. Conti had no involvement in the grant proceedings. The Division must now organise itself to examine the opposition.
statement: The composition is permissible because the Opposition Division consists of three technically qualified examiners and at least two of them (Dr. Brandt and Dr. Conti) did not take part in the grant proceedings.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An Opposition Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners, at least two of whom shall not have taken part in the proceedings for grant of the patent to which the opposition relates.

scenario: Helix Biotech holds European patent EP 3 000 111. An opposition is filed by a competitor. The EPO assigns an Opposition Division composed of three technically qualified examiners, Dr. Adler, Dr. Brandt and Dr. Conti. Dr. Adler had taken part in the examination proceedings that led to grant of the patent; Dr. Brandt and Dr. Conti had no involvement in the grant proceedings. The Division must now organise itself to examine the opposition.
statement: The composition is permissible because the Opposition Division consists of three technically qualified examiners and at least one of them (Dr. Brandt or Dr. Conti) did not take part in the grant proceedings.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: An Opposition Division shall consist of three technically qualified examiners, at least two of whom shall not have taken part in the proceedings for grant of the patent to which the opposition relates.

statement: When an appeal concerns a decision taken by an Opposition Division composed of four members, the Board of Appeal is made up of three technically qualified members and two legally qualified members.

statement: When an appeal concerns a decision taken by an Opposition Division composed of four members, the Board of Appeal is made up of two technically qualified members and one legally qualified member.

scenario: Helios Optics GmbH appeals a decision of an Examining Division that refused its European patent application. The decision was taken by an Examining Division consisting of three members. statement: The Board of Appeal hearing Helios Optics' appeal will consist of two technically qualified members and one legally qualified member.

scenario: Helios Optics GmbH appeals a decision of an Examining Division that refused its European patent application. The decision was taken by an Examining Division consisting of three members. statement: A decision of the Legal Division is taken by a single legally qualified member.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Decisions of the Legal Division shall be taken by one legally qualified member.

statement: A decision of the Legal Division is taken by a single technically qualified member.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Decisions of the Legal Division shall be taken by one legally qualified member.

scenario: Mr. Alvarez, a patent attorney, applies to be entered on the European list of professional representatives. statement: A person who has duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention enjoys, for filing a European patent application for the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

an application for a patent, a utility model or a utility certificate, or his successor in title, shall enjoy, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

The national office processed the filing such that it was sufficient to establish a date of filing, but the application was later refused on substantive grounds. On 1 March 2023, Helios filed a European patent application for the same invention and claimed priority from the earlier national filing.
statement: Helios may validly claim priority because its European patent application of 1 March 2023 was filed within the twelve-month priority period running from the 4 March 2022 first filing.

scenario: On 4 March 2022, Helios Optronics filed a patent application at the national patent office of a State party to the Paris Convention, disclosing a new lens-coating invention. statement: A European patent application may be filed jointly by several applicants, or alternatively by two or more applicants each designating different Contracting States.
label: TRUE

statement: A European patent application must be filed jointly by several applicants, or by two or more applicants each designating different Contracting States.

judge_deciding_span: may also be filed either by joint applicants or by two or more applicants designating different Contracting States.

scenario: NovaPharm GmbH and Dr. Keller jointly filed European patent application EP-1 on 10 March 2023. They wish to designate different Contracting States: NovaPharm GmbH designating Germany and France, and Dr. Keller designating Italy and Spain. They ask whether the EPC permits this arrangement of two applicants designating different Contracting States within a single European patent application.
statement: Under the EPC, a single European patent application may be filed by two or more applicants who designate different Contracting States, so NovaPharm and Dr. Keller may proceed as planned.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: A European patent application may also be filed either by joint applicants or by two or more applicants designating different Contracting States.

scenario: NovaPharm GmbH and Dr. Keller jointly filed European patent application EP-1 on 10 March 2023. They wish to designate different Contracting States: NovaPharm GmbH designating Germany and France, and Dr. Keller designating Italy and Spain. They ask whether the EPC permits this arrangement of two applicants designating different Contracting States within a single European patent application.
statement: Under the EPC, a single European patent application may be filed by two or more applicants only where they all designate the same Contracting States, so NovaPharm and Dr. Keller may not each designate different Contracting States.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: by two or more applicants designating different Contracting States

statement: A person who duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention may claim priority for a European patent application directed to the same invention during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of that first application.

Any person who has duly filed, in or for

an application for a patent, a utility model or a utility certificate, or his successor in title, shall enjoy, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

statement: A person who duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention may claim priority for a European patent application directed to the same invention during a period of six months from the date of filing of that first application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application

scenario: Helix Biotech duly filed its first patent application for a novel antibody in Japan (a WTO member and Paris Convention state) on 3 March 2023. Helix later wishes to file a European patent application for the same invention while validly claiming priority from the Japanese filing.
statement: Helix enjoys a right of priority for filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention during a period of twelve months from 3 March 2023, the date of filing of the Japanese first application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: shall enjoy, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

scenario: Helix Biotech duly filed its first patent application for a novel antibody in Japan (a WTO member and Paris Convention state) on 3 March 2023. Helix later wishes to file a European patent application for the same invention while validly claiming priority from the Japanese filing.

label: FALSE

statement: A European patent application must relate either to a single invention or to a group of inventions linked so as to form a single general inventive concept.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The European patent application shall relate to one invention only or to a group of inventions so linked as to form a single general inventive concept.

statement: A European patent application must relate either to a single invention or to a group of inventions linked so as to form a single general technical solution.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: single general inventive concept

scenario: Helix Diagnostics owns a granted European patent containing claims directed to two different chemical compounds that, the opponent argues, lack any common technical relationship and therefore do not form a single general inventive concept under Article 82 EPC. The opponent wishes to file an opposition relying solely on the ground that the patent does not relate to one invention only or a group of inventions linked by a single general inventive concept (i.e. lack of unity of invention).
statement: The opponent cannot validly base its opposition on lack of unity of invention under Article 82 EPC, because opposition may only be filed on the grounds exhaustively listed in Article 100 EPC, which do not include lack of unity.
label: TRUE

scenario: Helix Diagnostics owns a granted European patent containing claims directed to two different chemical compounds that, the opponent argues, lack any common technical relationship and therefore do not form a single general inventive concept under Article 82 EPC. The opponent wishes to file an opposition relying solely on the ground that the patent does not relate to one invention only or a group of inventions linked by a single general inventive concept (i.e. lack of unity of invention).
statement: The opponent can validly base its opposition on lack of unity of invention under Article 82 EPC, because that is one of the grounds on which opposition may be filed under Article 100 EPC.
label: FALSE

statement: Under Article 84 EPC, the claims must be clear and concise and supported by the description.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: They shall be clear and concise and be supported by the description.

statement: Under Article 84 EPC, the claims must be clear and concise and supported by the abstract.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: They shall be clear and concise and be supported by the description.

scenario: During opposition proceedings against Helios Optics' European patent EP-X, the opponent NovaLens argues that claim 1 is not clear and concise and therefore not supported by the description, citing this as a ground for revocation. The Opposition Division must decide whether lack of clarity, on its own, can be raised as a ground of opposition.
statement: NovaLens cannot rely on lack of clarity under Article 84 as a ground of opposition, because the grounds on which an opposition may be filed are exhaustively limited to those listed in Article 100 EPC.
label: TRUE

scenario: During opposition proceedings against Helios Optics' European patent EP-X, the opponent NovaLens argues that claim 1 is not clear and concise and therefore not supported by the description, citing this as a ground for revocation. The Opposition Division must decide whether lack of clarity, on its own, can be raised as a ground of opposition.
statement: NovaLens may rely on lack of clarity under Article 84 as a ground of opposition, since the grounds on which an opposition may be filed include any defect listed in Articles 84 and 100 EPC.
label: FALSE

statement: An opposition against a European patent can be based only on the grounds that the subject-matter is not patentable, that the patent insufficiently discloses the invention, or that the subject-matter extends beyond the content of the application as filed.
label: TRUE

statement: An opposition against a European patent can be based on the grounds that the subject-matter is not patentable, that the patent insufficiently discloses the invention, that the subject-matter extends beyond the content of the application as filed, or that the claims lack clarity.
label: FALSE

scenario: Helix Biotech holds a granted European patent EP 3 333 333 directed to a recombinant enzyme. A competitor, Vortex Pharma, wishes to file an opposition. Vortex believes (i) the patent claims subject-matter that lacks an inventive step, and (ii) the specification, as granted, fails to teach how to produce the enzyme reproducibly, so that a skilled person cannot carry out the invention. Vortex's patent attorney is checking whether these alleged defects are valid grounds on which an opposition may be based under the EPC.
statement: Vortex may base its opposition on the ground that the patent does not disclose the invention in a manner sufficiently clear and complete for it to be carried out by a person skilled in the art.
label: TRUE

scenario: Helix Biotech holds a granted European patent EP 3 333 333 directed to a recombinant enzyme. A competitor, Vortex Pharma, wishes to file an opposition. Vortex believes (i) the patent claims subject-matter that lacks an inventive step, and (ii) the specification, as granted, fails to teach how to produce the enzyme reproducibly, so that a skilled person cannot carry out the invention. Vortex's patent attorney is checking whether these alleged defects are valid grounds on which an opposition may be based under the EPC.
statement: Vortex may base its opposition on the ground that the patent's claims lack clarity, being insufficiently clear to delimit the scope of protection for a person skilled in the art.

statement: Under Article 54(2) EPC, the state of the art comprises everything made available to the public, by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The state of the art shall be held to comprise everything made available to the public by means of a written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.

statement: Under Article 54(2) EPC, the state of the art comprises everything made available to the public, by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of priority of the European patent application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: before the date of filing of the European patent application.

scenario: Helix Pharma files European patent application EP-A on 10 March 2022. A rival company, Genova Ltd, had filed an earlier European patent application EP-B on 5 January 2022, but EP-B was only published on 12 July 2023 (after Helix's filing date). EP-B as filed already discloses the very compound claimed in EP-A. The question is whether the content of EP-B counts as state of the art against EP-A for novelty purposes.
statement: Because EP-B was filed before Helix's filing date and published afterwards, its content as filed counts as state of the art against EP-A for assessing novelty.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the content of European patent applications as filed, the dates of filing of which are prior to the date referred to in paragraph 2 and which were published on or after that date, shall be considered as comprised in the state of the art.

scenario: Helix Pharma files European patent application EP-A on 10 March 2022. EP-B as filed already discloses the very compound claimed in EP-A. The question is whether the content of EP-B counts as state of the art against EP-A for novelty purposes.

statement: A legal person whose principal place of business is outside the Contracting States must be represented by a professional representative and act through him in all proceedings under the Convention, except when filing a European patent application.

scenario: Tanaka Robotics K.K. is a Japanese company with its principal place of business in Osaka, Japan, and no residence or place of business in any EPC Contracting State. It wishes to pursue a European patent application before the EPO.

statement: Only the applicant may validly pay the fees for a European patent application.

statement: Oral proceedings before the Boards of Appeal and the Enlarged Board of Appeal are public after publication of the European patent application, as are proceedings before the Opposition Divisions, subject to the possibility of excluding the public where its admission could cause serious and unjustified disadvantages.

statement: Oral proceedings before the Boards of Appeal and the Enlarged Board of Appeal are public after publication of the European patent application, as are proceedings before the Examining Divisions, subject to the possibility of excluding the public where its admission could cause serious and unjustified disadvantages.

scenario: Helix Therapeutics GmbH is the proprietor of European patent EP 3 999 000, which has been granted and published. A third party, Mr. Berner, files an opposition. topic: Article 117 – Means and taking of evidence

topic: Article 117 – Means and taking of evidence

scenario: In opposition proceedings against Helvar AB's European patent, opponent Northstar Oy alleges a public prior use of a lighting controller. To support this, Northstar files a written affidavit from its former engineer, Mr. Lindqvist, describing the alleged prior use. Helvar AB contests the alleged facts. Northstar does not offer Mr. Lindqvist to be heard as a witness, even though the opposition division indicates it cannot rely on the affidavit alone given the contestation.
statement: Because Helvar AB contests the alleged facts and Northstar Oy does not offer Mr. Lindqvist as a witness, the opposition division will not consider his written affidavit any further.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the person is not offered as a witness, the opposition division will not consider this evidence any further.

scenario: In opposition proceedings against Helvar AB's European patent, opponent Northstar Oy alleges a public prior use of a lighting controller. topic: Article 113 – Right to be heard and basis of decisions
scenario: Helios Optics GmbH filed European patent application EP-A under examination. label: TRUE

label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: At least two months' notice of a summons issued to a party, witness or expert to testify shall be given, unless they agree to a shorter period.

topic: Rule 118 – Summons to give evidence before the European Patent Office

On 3 March 2024 the EPO issues a summons to Dr. Holzer requiring her to testify. Dr. Holzer has not agreed to any shorter notice period. The summons sets out the relevant facts to be heard and informs her of certain procedural options.
statement: Because Dr. Holzer has not agreed to a shorter period, the summons issued to her must give at least two months' notice.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: At least two months' notice of a summons issued to a party, witness or expert to testify shall be given, unless they agree to a shorter period.

topic: Rule 118 – Summons to give evidence before the European Patent Office
scenario: During opposition proceedings concerning European patent EP 3 555 111, the Opposition Division of the EPO decides to hear a technical expert, Dr. Martina Holzer, who resides in Austria. label: FALSE

statement: A request for limitation or revocation of a European patent may be filed at any time after grant, with no restriction on the period between grant and the filing of the request, including even after the patent has expired.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Unlike in the opposition procedure, there is no restriction on the period between the grant of the patent and the filing of the request. Accordingly, the request can be filed at any time after grant, after opposition proceedings or even after expiry of the patent.

statement: A request for limitation or revocation of a European patent may be filed only within a fixed period after grant, since a restriction applies to the period between grant and the filing of the request, and it can no longer be filed once the patent has expired.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Unlike in the opposition procedure, there is no restriction on the period between the grant of the patent and the filing of the request. Accordingly, the request can be filed at any time after grant, after opposition proceedings or even after expiry of the patent.

scenario: Helix Pharma AG holds European patent EP 3 210 987, granted on 15 March 2018. The opposition period expired on 15 December 2018 with no opposition filed, and the patent later lapsed on 30 June 2023. On 10 September 2023, after the patent had already expired, Helix Pharma decides it wants to narrow the scope of its claims and considers filing a request for limitation with the EPO.
statement: Helix Pharma AG may validly file its request for limitation in September 2023, because there is no restriction on the period between grant and filing, and a limitation request can be filed even after the patent has expired.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the request can be filed at any time after grant, after opposition proceedings or even after expiry of the patent.

scenario: Helix Pharma AG holds European patent EP 3 210 987, granted on 15 March 2018. The opposition period expired on 15 December 2018 with no opposition filed, and the patent later lapsed on 30 June 2023. On 10 September 2023, after the patent had already expired, Helix Pharma decides it wants to narrow the scope of its claims and considers filing a request for limitation with the EPO.
statement: Helix Pharma AG cannot validly file its request for limitation in September 2023, because a limitation request must be filed before the patent expires.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the request can be filed at any time after grant, after opposition proceedings or even after expiry of the patent.

statement: If opposition proceedings concerning a European patent are pending when a request for limitation or revocation is filed, that request is deemed not to have been filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The request for limitation or revocation shall be deemed not to have been filed if opposition proceedings in respect of the patent are pending at the time of filing the request.

statement: If opposition proceedings concerning a European patent are pending when a request for limitation or revocation is filed, the request is suspended until the opposition proceedings have been concluded.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The request for limitation or revocation shall be deemed not to have been filed if opposition proceedings in respect of the patent are pending at the time of filing the request.

scenario: PharmaCorp owns European patent EP 3 000 111. On 10 March 2024 a third party validly files an opposition against the patent, and those opposition proceedings are still pending. On 5 June 2024, while the opposition is pending, PharmaCorp files a request for limitation of the same patent with the EPO.
statement: Because opposition proceedings against EP 3 000 111 were pending when PharmaCorp filed its limitation request, that limitation request is deemed not to have been filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The request for limitation or revocation shall be deemed not to have been filed if opposition proceedings in respect of the patent are pending at the time of filing the request.

scenario: PharmaCorp owns European patent EP 3 000 111. On 10 March 2024 a third party validly files an opposition against the patent, and those opposition proceedings are still pending. On 5 June 2024, while the opposition is pending, PharmaCorp files a request for limitation of the same patent with the EPO.
statement: Because opposition proceedings against EP 3 000 111 were pending when PharmaCorp filed its limitation request, the EPO must terminate the opposition proceedings and proceed with the limitation request.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The request for limitation or revocation shall be deemed not to have been filed if opposition proceedings in respect of the patent are pending at the time of filing the request.

statement: Where a request for limitation is allowable, the Examining Division invites the requester to file a translation of the amended claims into the EPO official languages other than the language of the proceedings within a period of three months.
label: TRUE

statement: Where a request for limitation is allowable, the Examining Division invites the requester to file a translation of the amended claims into the EPO official languages other than the language of the proceedings within a period of two months.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of three months

scenario: Helix Pharma AG owns European patent EP 3 210 000. It files a request to limit the claims. The Examining Division finds the request admissible and, after examination, concludes that the amended claims are allowable under Rule 95(2). The Examining Division communicates this to Helix Pharma and invites it to file a translation of the amended claims into the EPO's other two official languages.
statement: The Examining Division must invite Helix Pharma to file the translation of the amended claims within a period of three months.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of three months

scenario: Helix Pharma AG owns European patent EP 3 210 000. It files a request to limit the claims. The Examining Division finds the request admissible and, after examination, concludes that the amended claims are allowable under Rule 95(2). The Examining Division communicates this to Helix Pharma and invites it to file a translation of the amended claims into the EPO's other two official languages.
statement: The Examining Division must invite Helix Pharma to file the translation of the amended claims within a period of two months.
label: FALSE

statement: Where a European application claims multiple priorities, the earliest of the previous applications must have been filed no more than 12 months before the European application's filing date.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: in all cases the earliest application must have been filed not more than 12 months before the date of filing of the European application.

statement: Where a European application claims multiple priorities, the earliest of the previous applications must have been filed no more than 6 months before the European application's filing date.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: in all cases the earliest application must have been filed not more than 12 months before the date of filing of the European application.

scenario: TechCo files a European patent application on 1 March 2023 that describes and claims two embodiments of a sensor: embodiment A (an infrared variant) and embodiment B (an ultrasonic variant). Embodiment A was first disclosed in a French application filed on 10 June 2022, and embodiment B was first disclosed in a German application filed on 5 September 2022. Both earlier applications were filed within the 12 months preceding the European filing. TechCo claims priority from both earlier applications. The two embodiments are claimed as alternatives in a single claim.
statement: Because embodiments A and B are claimed as alternatives in a single claim, embodiment A enjoys the French priority date (10 June 2022) and embodiment B enjoys the German priority date (5 September 2022) as their respective effective dates.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: embodiment A will have the French priority date and embodiment B the German priority date as effective dates. If embodiments A and B are claimed as alternatives in one claim, these alternatives will likewise have the different priority dates as effective dates.

scenario: TechCo files a European patent application on 1 March 2023 that describes and claims two embodiments of a sensor: embodiment A (an infrared variant) and embodiment B (an ultrasonic variant). Embodiment A was first disclosed in a French application filed on 10 June 2022, and embodiment B was first disclosed in a German application filed on 5 September 2022. Both earlier applications were filed within the 12 months preceding the European filing. TechCo claims priority from both earlier applications. The two embodiments are claimed as alternatives in a single claim.
statement: Because embodiments A and B are claimed as alternatives in a single claim, both alternatives must share a single effective date, namely the date of the earliest priority application (10 June 2022).
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: embodiment A will have the French priority date and embodiment B the German priority date as effective dates. If embodiments A and B are claimed as alternatives in one claim, these alternatives will likewise have the different priority dates as effective dates.

statement: A claim is entitled to the priority date only if its subject-matter is directly and unambiguously derivable from the disclosure of the invention in the priority document, considering features implicit to the skilled person.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the subject-matter of the claim must be directly and unambiguously derivable from the disclosure of the invention in the priority document, also considering any features implicit to a skilled person in what is expressly mentioned in the document

statement: A claim is entitled to the priority date only if its subject-matter is directly and unambiguously derivable from the disclosure of the invention in the priority document, considering features implicit to the skilled person, even where the priority document refers to that subject-matter only in broad and general terms.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: it is not sufficient if the subject-matter in question is merely referred to in broad and general terms.

scenario: Helix Tools GmbH filed a first application (the priority document) on 3 March 2022. Its description disclosed a hand drill incorporating a "releasable fastening means", and the description illustrated three distinct embodiments of such releasable fastening elements: a nut and bolt, a spring catch, and a toggle-operated latch. None of these were mentioned in any of the priority document's claims. On 1 March 2023, Helix filed a European patent application claiming priority, with claim 1 directed to the same drill including a "releasable fastening means". The examiner must decide whether claim 1 is entitled to the priority date.
statement: Claim 1 can validly enjoy the priority date even though the releasable fastening means was not recited in any of the priority document's claims, because it is sufficient that the previous application's documents taken as a whole specifically disclose the subject-matter.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: It is sufficient if the documents of the previous application, taken as a whole, "specifically disclose" such subject-matter.

scenario: Helix Tools GmbH filed a first application (the priority document) on 3 March 2022. Its description disclosed a hand drill incorporating a "releasable fastening means", and the description illustrated three distinct embodiments of such releasable fastening elements: a nut and bolt, a spring catch, and a toggle-operated latch. None of these were mentioned in any of the priority document's claims. On 1 March 2023, Helix filed a European patent application claiming priority, with claim 1 directed to the same drill including a "releasable fastening means". The examiner must decide whether claim 1 is entitled to the priority date.
statement: Claim 1 can validly enjoy the priority date only if the releasable fastening means was recited in at least one of the priority document's claims, since subject-matter must appear in the previous application's claims to support priority.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: It is not necessary for the subject-matter for which priority is claimed to appear in any of the previous application's claims.

topic: 5.1 Determination of the closest prior art
statement: The closest prior art is to be assessed from the perspective of the skilled person as of the day before the filing or priority date that is valid for the claimed invention.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The closest prior art must be assessed from the skilled person's point of view on the day before the filing or priority date valid for the claimed invention.

topic: 5.1 Determination of the closest prior art
statement: The closest prior art is to be assessed from the perspective of the skilled person as of the day after the filing or priority date that is valid for the claimed invention.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The closest prior art must be assessed from the skilled person's point of view on the day before the filing or priority date valid for the claimed invention.

topic: 5.1 Determination of the closest prior art
scenario: Helix Bio filed a European patent application for a blood-glucose sensor. During examination, the examiner relied on document D3, a document concerning a glucose sensor for a similar use, and showed that the claimed sensor was obvious starting from D3. In response, Helix Bio argued that D5, a document in a much more remote technical field, was actually a more promising springboard and that the invention would be non-obvious if D5 were used as the starting point.
statement: Helix Bio cannot defeat the lack-of-inventive-step objection by arguing that D5 is a more promising springboard, since a document from which the invention appears non-obvious cannot be closer than one from which it appears obvious.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the applicant or proprietor cannot refute the argument that the claimed subject-matter lacks inventive step by submitting that a more promising springboard is available: a piece of prior art on the basis of which the claimed invention is considered non-obvious cannot be "closer" than a document on the basis of which the claimed invention appears obvious

topic: 5.1 Determination of the closest prior art
scenario: Helix Bio filed a European patent application for a blood-glucose sensor. During examination, the examiner relied on document D3, a document concerning a glucose sensor for a similar use, and showed that the claimed sensor was obvious starting from D3. In response, Helix Bio argued that D5, a document in a much more remote technical field, was actually a more promising springboard and that the invention would be non-obvious if D5 were used as the starting point.
statement: Helix Bio can defeat the lack-of-inventive-step objection by arguing that D5 is a more promising springboard, since a document from which the invention appears non-obvious cannot be closer than one from which it appears obvious.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the applicant or proprietor cannot refute the argument that the claimed subject-matter lacks inventive step by submitting that a more promising springboard is available: a piece of prior art on the basis of which the claimed invention is considered non-obvious cannot be "closer" than a document on the basis of which the claimed invention appears obvious

statement: When a claimed invention solves several independent partial problems, each partial problem is assessed separately, and the claim is inventive as long as at least one of the combinations of features solving a partial problem involves an inventive step.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Indeed, in such a case it is necessary to separately assess, for each partial problem, whether the combination of features solving the partial problem is obviously derivable from the prior art. This means that a different document can be combined with the closest prior art for each partial problem (see T 389/86). For the subject-matter of the claim to be inventive, it suffices, however, that one of these combinations of features involves an inventive step.

statement: When a claimed invention solves several independent partial problems, each partial problem is assessed separately, and the claim is inventive only if every one of the combinations of features solving the partial problems involves an inventive step.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: For the subject-matter of the claim to be inventive, it suffices, however, that one of these combinations of features involves an inventive step.

scenario: Acme Ltd files a European patent application claiming a coffee machine. During examination, the examiner identifies document D1 as the closest prior art, a coffee machine lacking two features of the claim. The examiner finds that the claim solves two clearly independent partial problems: (a) reducing energy consumption and (b) improving milk frothing. D2 obviously solves partial problem (a) when combined with D1, but no document renders the combination solving partial problem (b) obvious. The examiner must decide whether the claim as a whole involves an inventive step.
statement: Because the claim solves several independent partial problems, it is enough for the claim to be inventive that one of the combinations of features solving a partial problem involves an inventive step, so the existence of an inventive partial-problem solution can support inventive step of the whole claim.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: For the subject-matter of the claim to be inventive, it suffices, however, that one of these combinations of features involves an inventive step.

scenario: Acme Ltd files a European patent application claiming a coffee machine. During examination, the examiner identifies document D1 as the closest prior art, a coffee machine lacking two features of the claim. The examiner finds that the claim solves two clearly independent partial problems: (a) reducing energy consumption and (b) improving milk frothing. D2 obviously solves partial problem (a) when combined with D1, but no document renders the combination solving partial problem (b) obvious. The examiner must decide whether the claim as a whole involves an inventive step.
statement: Because the claim solves several independent partial problems, the claim is inventive only if all of the combinations of features solving the partial problems involve an inventive step, so a single inventive partial-problem solution is not enough.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: For the subject-matter of the claim to be inventive, it suffices, however, that one of these combinations of features involves an inventive step.

topic: Rule 38 – Filing fee and search fee

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
statement: A European patent specification is published in the language of the proceedings and must include a translation of the claims into the other two official languages of the EPO.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Specifications of European patents shall be published in the language of the proceedings and shall include a translation of the claims in the other two official languages of the European Patent Office.

label: FALSE

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
scenario: Mireille, a French national resident in France, files a European patent application directly with the EPO. Because French is an official language of the European Patent Office, she files her application in French and intends to rely on French as the language of the proceedings throughout examination.
statement: Because Mireille filed her application in French, which is one of the three official languages of the EPO (English, French and German), French may serve as the language of the proceedings.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span:
(1) The official languages of the European Patent Office shall be English, French and German.
(2) A European patent application shall be filed in one of the official languages or, if filed in any other language, translated into one of the official languages in accordance with the Implementing Regulations. Throughout the proceedings before the European Patent Office, such translation may be brought into conformity with the application as filed. If a required translation is not filed in due time, the application shall be deemed to be withdrawn.
(3) The official language of the European Patent Office in which the European patent application is filed or into which it is translated shall be used as the language of the proceedings in all proceedings before the European Patent Office, unless the Implementing Regulations provide otherwise.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents

statement: By virtue of the right of priority, the priority date is treated as the filing date of the European patent application for the purposes of Article 54(2), Article 54(3) and Article 60(2) EPC.

scenario: Helix Bio filed a European patent application on 10 March 2023, validly claiming priority from an earlier application filed on 12 May 2022. judge_deciding_span: the date of priority shall count as the date of filing of the European patent application for the purposes of Article 54, paragraphs 2

scenario: Helix Bio filed a European patent application on 10 March 2023, validly claiming priority from an earlier application filed on 12 May 2022. label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the date of priority shall count as the date of filing of the European patent application for the purposes of Article 54, paragraphs 2
