statement: Under Art. 123(2), an amendment may not add subject-matter that the skilled person cannot directly and unambiguously derive from the application as filed, but literal support in the original text is not required for the amendment to be allowable.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Under Art. 123(2), it is impermissible to add to a European application subject-matter which the skilled person cannot derive directly and unambiguously, using common general knowledge and also taking into account any features implicit to a person skilled in the art in what is expressly mentioned in the document, from the disclosure of the application as filed. Literal support is, however, not required by the wording of Art. 123(2)

statement: Under Art. 123(2), an amendment may not add subject-matter that the skilled person cannot directly and unambiguously derive from the application as filed, and literal support in the original text is required for the amendment to be allowable.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Literal support is, however, not required by the wording of Art. 123(2)

scenario: Helix Biotech filed European patent application EP-A with a description and claims disclosing a polymer coating. During examination, Helix amends claim 1 to add a feature specifying a coating thickness of 50 micrometres. This exact numerical value is not stated anywhere in the application as filed, nor is it the clear and unambiguous consequence of what is explicitly mentioned, even taking into account the common general knowledge of the skilled person. Helix argues the amendment should be allowed because the application generally discusses 'thin coatings'.
statement: The amendment is not allowable, because it presents the skilled person with information that is not directly and unambiguously derivable from the application as filed, even taking implicit matter into account.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An amendment is regarded as introducing subject-matter which extends beyond the content of the application as filed, and therefore unallowable, if the overall change in the content of the application (whether by way of addition, alteration or excision) results in the skilled person being presented with information which is not directly and unambiguously derivable from that previously presented by the application, even when account is taken of matter which is implicit to a person skilled in the art

scenario: Helix Biotech filed European patent application EP-A with a description and claims disclosing a polymer coating. During examination, Helix amends claim 1 to add a feature specifying a coating thickness of 50 micrometres. This exact numerical value is not stated anywhere in the application as filed, nor is it the clear and unambiguous consequence of what is explicitly mentioned, even taking into account the common general knowledge of the skilled person. Helix argues the amendment should be allowed because the application generally discusses 'thin coatings'.
statement: The amendment is allowable, because it presents the skilled person with information that would be rendered obvious by the application as filed in light of common general knowledge.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: However, the question of what may be rendered obvious by that disclosure in the light of common general knowledge is not relevant to the assessment of what is implicitly disclosed by that document

statement: Under Article 85 EPC, the abstract serves the purpose of technical information only and may not be taken into account for interpreting the scope of the protection sought.

statement: Under Article 85 EPC, the abstract serves the purpose of technical information only and may be taken into account for interpreting the scope of the protection sought.

statement: Under Article 123(3) EPC, a granted European patent cannot be amended so as to broaden the scope of protection it confers.
label: TRUE

statement: Under Article 123(3) EPC, a European patent application cannot be amended so as to broaden the scope of protection it confers.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The European patent may not be amended in such a way as to extend the protection it confers.

scenario: Helix Polymers GmbH holds a granted European patent EP-X directed to a sealing compound. During opposition proceedings, Helix wants to amend granted claim 1 by broadening the claimed range of a curing temperature, so that the amended claim would cover embodiments not covered by the granted claim. The opposition division must assess whether this amendment is allowable under Article 123(3) EPC.
statement: Helix's proposed amendment broadening the granted claim to cover embodiments not previously covered must be refused, because a granted European patent may not be amended so as to extend the protection it confers.

statement: Under Article 123(3) EPC, a granted European patent cannot be amended so as to broaden the scope of protection it confers.
label: TRUE

statement: Under Article 123(3) EPC, a European patent application cannot be amended so as to broaden the scope of protection it confers.

scenario: Helia Materials GmbH holds granted European patent EP 3 555 111, whose granted claim 1 covers "a polymer coating comprising additive X in an amount of 5 to 10 wt%". During opposition proceedings, Helia files an amended claim 1 reciting "a polymer coating comprising additive X in an amount of 3 to 15 wt%", arguing the broader range was disclosed in a dependent claim of the application as filed.
statement: Helia's proposed amendment is not allowable because broadening the granted range from 5 to 10 wt% to 3 to 15 wt% extends the protection conferred by the patent, which is prohibited regardless of what the application as filed disclosed.
label: TRUE

scenario: Helia Materials GmbH holds granted European patent EP 3 555 111, whose granted claim 1 covers "a polymer coating comprising additive X in an amount of 5 to 10 wt%". label: FALSE

statement: A European patent application may not be amended so that it contains subject-matter extending beyond the content of the application as filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The European patent application or European patent may not be amended in such a way that it contains subject-matter which extends beyond the content of the application as filed.

statement: A European patent application may not be amended so that it contains subject-matter extending beyond the content of the priority application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The European patent application or European patent may not be amended in such a way that it contains subject-matter which extends beyond the content of the application as filed.

scenario: Helvetica Polymers AG files European patent application EP-X disclosing a coating composition. During examination, the examiner finds that one embodiment (a low-temperature curing variant) is insufficiently disclosed, while three other embodiments are fully and sufficiently described. To overcome the objection, Helvetica proposes to add a worked numerical example and an extra processing feature to the application, neither of which appeared anywhere in the application as originally filed, in order to make the low-temperature embodiment sufficiently disclosed.
statement: Helvetica cannot remedy the insufficiency by adding the previously undisclosed example and feature, since doing so would introduce subject-matter extending beyond the content of the application as filed and thus offend Art. 123(2) EPC.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: that deficiency cannot subsequently be remedied by adding further examples or features without offending Art. 123(2), which requires that amendments may not result in the introduction of subject-matter extending beyond the content of the application as filed

topic: Rule 80 – Amendment of the European patent

label: TRUE

topic: Rule 80 – Amendment of the European patent
scenario: Helix Bio GmbH is the proprietor of European patent EP 3 210 555, which was opposed by Nordics AG on the sole ground of lack of inventive step under Article 100(a). During the opposition proceedings, Helix Bio notices that one of its dependent claims arguably extends beyond the content of the application as filed, an issue falling under Article 100(c), which Nordics AG never raised. Helix Bio files amended claims to address this added-matter problem.
statement: Helix Bio's amendment is admissible because it is occasioned by a ground for opposition under Article 100, even though Nordics AG never invoked the added-matter ground.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the description, claims and drawings may be amended, provided that the amendments are occasioned by a ground for opposition under Article 100, even if that ground has not been invoked by the opponent.

topic: Rule 80 – Amendment of the European patent
scenario: Helix Bio GmbH is the proprietor of European patent EP 3 210 555, which was opposed by Nordics AG on the sole ground of lack of inventive step under Article 100(a). Helix Bio files amended claims to address this added-matter problem.

topic: Rule 38 – Filing fee and search fee

topic: Rule 38 – Filing fee and search fee

The Rules relating to Fees provide for an additional fee as part of the filing fee because the application comprises more than 35 pages. Helios filed its first set of claims and the certified copy of the priority document together with the application on 14 March 2023.
statement: Helios must pay the filing fee and the search fee within one month of filing the European patent application, i.e. counting from 14 March 2023.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The filing fee and search fee shall be paid within one month of filing the European patent application.

topic: Rule 38 – Filing fee and search fee

label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The filing fee and search fee shall be paid within one month of filing the European patent application.

statement: Under Rule 134(5) EPC, a late document is treated as received in due time if the party proves a dislocation due to an exceptional occurrence on any of the ten days before the period expired, provided mailing was effected no later than the fifth day after the dislocation ended.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: a party concerned may produce evidence that on any of the ten days preceding the day of expiry of a period the delivery or transmission of mail was dislocated due to an exceptional occurrence such as a natural disaster, war, civil disorder, a general breakdown in any of the means of electronic communication permitted by the President of the European Patent Office under Rule 2, paragraph 1, or other like reasons affecting the locality where the party or their representative resides or has their place of business. If the evidence produced satisfies the European Patent Office, a document received late shall be deemed to have been received in due time, provided that the mailing or the transmission was effected at the latest on the fifth day after the end of the dislocation.

statement: Under Rule 134(5) EPC, a late document is treated as received in due time if the party proves a dislocation due to an exceptional occurrence on any of the five days before the period expired, provided mailing was effected no later than the fifth day after the dislocation ended.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: on any of the ten days preceding the day of expiry of a period

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd, a company resident in Spain with a representative whose place of business is in Spain, has a period that expires on Saturday 15 June 2024, a day on which the EPO filing offices are not open for receipt of documents. There is no general dislocation of mail. The next day on which all filing offices are open and mail is delivered is Monday 17 June 2024.
statement: Because the period expires on a day on which an EPO filing office is not open for receipt of documents, it is extended to the first day thereafter on which all the filing offices are open and mail is delivered.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the period shall extend to the first day thereafter on which all the filing offices are open for receipt of documents and on which mail is delivered.

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd, a company resident in Spain with a representative whose place of business is in Spain, has a period that expires on Saturday 15 June 2024, a day on which the EPO filing offices are not open for receipt of documents. There is no general dislocation of mail. The next day on which all filing offices are open and mail is delivered is Monday 17 June 2024.
statement: Because the period expires on a day on which an EPO filing office is not open for receipt of documents, it is extended to the first day thereafter on which any one of the filing offices is open and mail is delivered.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the period shall extend to the first day thereafter on which all the filing offices are open for receipt of documents and on which mail is delivered.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents

label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: if filed in a language other than an official EPO language (English, French and German), a translation into one of the official languages must be filed within two months of the date of filing

topic: Rule 57 – Examination as to formal requirements

label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the European patent application does not comply with the requirements of Rule 57(a) to Rule 57(d), Rule 57(h) and Rule 57(i), the European Patent Office shall inform the applicant accordingly and invite them to correct the deficiencies noted within two months.

topic: Rule 57 – Examination as to formal requirements

topic: Rule 57 – Examination as to formal requirements

topic: Rule 57 – Examination as to formal requirements

topic: Rule 58 – Correction of deficiencies in the application documents

topic: Article 58 – Entitlement to file a European patent application

topic: Article 58 – Entitlement to file a European patent application

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor
scenario: NovaChem AG filed a European patent application designating Dr. Aldous Penrose as the sole inventor. It later emerges that Dr. Penrose was wrongly named and that the true inventor is Dr. Bianca Holm. NovaChem AG, as applicant, files a request with the EPO to rectify the designation of the inventor, replacing Dr. Penrose with Dr. Holm.
statement: NovaChem AG's request to rectify the designation can be granted only if the consent of the newly designated Dr. Holm is obtained.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: only with the consent of the wrongly designated person

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor
statement: Rectification of an incorrect designation of an inventor requires a request together with the consent of the wrongly designated person, and additionally the consent of the applicant or proprietor where the request is filed by a third party.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An incorrect designation of an inventor shall be rectified upon request and only with the consent of the wrongly designated person and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent.

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor
statement: Rectification of an incorrect designation of an inventor requires only a request together with the consent of the wrongly designated person, with no need for the applicant's or proprietor's consent even where the request is filed by a third party.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor
scenario: NovaTech GmbH filed a European patent application that designated Dr. Eva Lindqvist as inventor. It later turns out that Dr. Lindqvist was never an inventor and that the correct inventor is Dr. Marcus Ohlsson. NovaTech, the applicant, files a request with the EPO to rectify the designation of inventor, removing Dr. Lindqvist and substituting Dr. Ohlsson.
statement: The EPO may rectify the designation only if the request is accompanied by the consent of Dr. Lindqvist, the wrongly designated person.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An incorrect designation may be rectified, provided that a request is received together with the consent of the wrongly designated person

topic: Rule 21 – Rectification of the designation of an inventor
scenario: NovaTech GmbH filed a European patent application that designated Dr. Eva Lindqvist as inventor. It later turns out that Dr. Lindqvist was never an inventor and that the correct inventor is Dr. Marcus Ohlsson. NovaTech, the applicant, files a request with the EPO to rectify the designation of inventor, removing Dr. Lindqvist and substituting Dr. Ohlsson.
statement: The EPO may rectify the designation only if the request is accompanied by the consent of Dr. Ohlsson, the correctly named new inventor.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: An incorrect designation may be rectified, provided that a request is received together with the consent of the wrongly designated person

statement: Rectification of an incorrect inventor designation requires a request together with the consent of the wrongly designated person, and additionally the consent of the applicant or proprietor when the request is filed by a third party.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: An incorrect designation of an inventor shall be rectified upon request and only with the consent of the wrongly designated person and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent.

statement: Rectification of an incorrect inventor designation requires a request together with the consent of the wrongly designated person, and additionally the consent of the applicant or proprietor when the request is filed by a third party, as well as the consent of the previously designated inventors.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: only with the consent of the wrongly designated person and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent

scenario: Helios Solar GmbH filed a European patent application that named Dr. Anna Vogt as the sole inventor. It later emerged that Dr. Vogt had nothing to do with the invention and that the true inventor was Mr. Lukas Brandt. A third party, unrelated to the application, files a request with the EPO to rectify the incorrect designation of Dr. Vogt. The third party obtains Dr. Vogt's consent to the rectification but does not approach Helios Solar GmbH at all.
statement: Because the request was filed by a third party, the rectification requires not only Dr. Vogt's consent but also the consent of the applicant, Helios Solar GmbH.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: only with the consent of the wrongly designated person and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent

scenario: Helios Solar GmbH filed a European patent application that named Dr. Anna Vogt as the sole inventor. It later emerged that Dr. Vogt had nothing to do with the invention and that the true inventor was Mr. Lukas Brandt. A third party, unrelated to the application, files a request with the EPO to rectify the incorrect designation of Dr. Vogt. The third party obtains Dr. Vogt's consent to the rectification but does not approach Helios Solar GmbH at all.
statement: Because the request was filed by a third party, the rectification requires only Dr. Vogt's consent, and the consent of the applicant Helios Solar GmbH is not needed.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: only with the consent of the wrongly designated person and, where such a request is filed by a third party, the consent of the applicant for or proprietor of the patent

statement: An opposition against a European patent can be based only on the grounds enumerated in Article 100 EPC, so an opposition asserting a ground outside that list is not admissible on that ground.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Opposition may only be filed on the grounds that:

statement: An opposition against a European patent can be based on any ground the opponent considers relevant, including grounds not enumerated in Article 100 EPC.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Opposition may only be filed on the grounds that:

scenario: Helvetia Pharma AG holds a granted European patent for a slow-release tablet. A competitor, NordMed ApS, wants to file an opposition. NordMed's patent attorney reviews the granted patent and considers three possible attacks: (1) the claimed tablet lacks novelty and inventive step over a prior publication; (2) the patent specification fails to enable a skilled person to reproduce the coating process; and (3) granted claim 4 contains a feature that was never present in the application as originally filed. NordMed asks whether lack of clarity of granted claim 2 — which it considers ambiguously worded — can also serve as a standalone ground in the opposition.
statement: NordMed's first three intended attacks (unpatentability, insufficient disclosure, and added subject-matter) are admissible opposition grounds, but lack of clarity of a granted claim is not by itself a ground on which opposition may be filed.
label: TRUE

scenario: Helvetia Pharma AG holds a granted European patent for a slow-release tablet. A competitor, NordMed ApS, wants to file an opposition. NordMed's patent attorney reviews the granted patent and considers three possible attacks: (1) the claimed tablet lacks novelty and inventive step over a prior publication; (2) the patent specification fails to enable a skilled person to reproduce the coating process; and (3) granted claim 4 contains a feature that was never present in the application as originally filed. NordMed asks whether lack of clarity of granted claim 2 — which it considers ambiguously worded — can also serve as a standalone ground in the opposition.
statement: NordMed's first three intended attacks (unpatentability, insufficient disclosure, and added subject-matter) are admissible opposition grounds, and lack of clarity of a granted claim is likewise a ground on which opposition may be filed.
label: FALSE

topic: Rule 56 – Missing parts of the description or missing drawings
statement: An applicant may withdraw filed missing parts of the description or missing drawings within one month of the notification under Rule 56(2) or (5), last sentence, with the effect that the re-dating is deemed not to have been made.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Within one month of the notification referred to in paragraph 2 or paragraph 5, last sentence, the applicant may withdraw the missing parts of the description or the missing drawings filed, in which case the re-dating shall be deemed not to have been made.

topic: Rule 56 – Missing parts of the description or missing drawings
statement: An applicant may withdraw filed missing parts of the description or missing drawings within two months of the notification under Rule 56(2) or (5), last sentence, with the effect that the re-dating is deemed not to have been made.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Within one month of the notification referred to in paragraph 2 or paragraph 5, last sentence, the applicant may withdraw the missing parts of the description or the missing drawings filed, in which case the re-dating shall be deemed not to have been made.

topic: Rule 56 – Missing parts of the description or missing drawings
scenario: On 10 March 2024, GeoTech Ltd files a European patent application without claiming any priority. The description refers to "Figure 3", but no drawings are filed with the application. The EPO issues a communication under Rule 56(1) on 2 April 2024 inviting GeoTech to file the missing drawings. GeoTech files the missing drawing of Figure 3 on 20 May 2024, i.e. within two months of that communication. The drawing was not contained in any earlier application.
statement: Because GeoTech filed the missing drawing within two months of the Rule 56(1) communication and claims no priority, the application will be re-dated to 20 May 2024, the date on which the missing drawing was filed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If missing parts of the description or missing drawings are filed later than the date of filing, but within two months of the date of filing or, if a communication is issued under paragraph 1 or under Rule 56a, paragraph 1, within two months of that communication, the application shall be re-dated to the date on which the missing parts of the description or missing drawings were filed.

topic: Rule 56 – Missing parts of the description or missing drawings
scenario: On 10 March 2024, GeoTech Ltd files a European patent application without claiming any priority. The description refers to "Figure 3", but no drawings are filed with the application. The EPO issues a communication under Rule 56(1) on 2 April 2024 inviting GeoTech to file the missing drawings. GeoTech files the missing drawing of Figure 3 on 20 May 2024, i.e. within two months of that communication. The drawing was not contained in any earlier application.
statement: Because GeoTech filed the missing drawing within two months of the Rule 56(1) communication and claims no priority, the application will be re-dated to 2 April 2024, the date on which the Rule 56(1) communication was issued.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the application shall be re-dated to the date on which the missing parts of the description or missing drawings were filed

topic: Rule 56a – Erroneously filed application documents or parts
statement: An applicant may withdraw correct application documents or parts that were filed within one month of the notification under Rule 56a, paragraph 3 or paragraph 6, and the re-dating is then deemed not to have been made.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Within one month of the notification referred to in paragraph 3 or paragraph 6, last sentence, the applicant may withdraw the correct application documents or parts filed, in which case the re-dating shall be deemed not to have been made.

topic: Rule 56a – Erroneously filed application documents or parts
statement: An applicant may withdraw correct application documents or parts that were filed within two months of the notification under Rule 56a, paragraph 3 or paragraph 6, and the re-dating is then deemed not to have been made.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Within one month of the notification referred to in paragraph 3 or paragraph 6, last sentence, the applicant may withdraw the correct application documents or parts filed, in which case the re-dating shall be deemed not to have been made.

topic: Rule 56a – Erroneously filed application documents or parts
scenario: Helios Optics GmbH filed European patent application EP-X on 3 March 2025. The EPO's examination under Article 90(1) revealed that the description appeared to have been erroneously filed (it described a completely different invention). On 10 March 2025 the EPO issued a communication under Rule 56a(1). On 2 April 2025 Helios filed the correct description, which is completely contained in an earlier French application whose priority EP-X validly claims. Together with the correct description, Helios requested that the filing date be maintained and, within the same period, filed a copy of the earlier application, a French-to-English translation, and an indication of where the correct description is contained in the earlier application.
statement: Because Helios filed the correct description within the period together with the request, a copy of the earlier application, a translation, and an indication of where the correct part is contained, the date of filing of EP-X is maintained and the erroneously filed description remains in the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If these requirements are fulfilled, the correct application documents or parts shall be included in the application and the erroneously filed application documents or parts shall remain in the application.

topic: Rule 56a – Erroneously filed application documents or parts
scenario: Helios Optics GmbH filed European patent application EP-X on 3 March 2025. The EPO's examination under Article 90(1) revealed that the description appeared to have been erroneously filed (it described a completely different invention). On 10 March 2025 the EPO issued a communication under Rule 56a(1). On 2 April 2025 Helios filed the correct description, which is completely contained in an earlier French application whose priority EP-X validly claims. Together with the correct description, Helios requested that the filing date be maintained and, within the same period, filed a copy of the earlier application, a French-to-English translation, and an indication of where the correct description is contained in the earlier application.
statement: Because Helios filed the correct description within the period together with the request, a copy of the earlier application, a translation, and an indication of where the correct part is contained, the date of filing of EP-X is maintained and the erroneously filed description is deemed not to have been filed.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the erroneously filed application documents or parts shall remain in the application

statement: A person who has duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention enjoys a right of priority, for the purpose of filing a European patent application for the same invention, during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span:
Any person who has duly filed, in or for
(a) any State party to the Paris Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property or
(b) any Member of the World Trade Organization,
an application for a patent, a utility model or a utility certificate, or his successor in title, shall enjoy, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

statement: A person who has duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention enjoys a right of priority, for the purpose of filing a European patent application for the same invention, during a period of six months from the date of filing of the first application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd duly filed a national patent application for a new lens coating with the patent office of a State party to the Paris Convention on 3 March 2023. Helios then prepared a European patent application directed to the same invention.
statement: Helios enjoys a right of priority for its European application during a period of twelve months running from 3 March 2023, the filing date of its first application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: shall enjoy, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

statement: A person who has duly filed a patent application in a State party to the Paris Convention enjoys, for the purpose of filing a European patent application in respect of the same invention, a right of priority during a period of twelve months from the date of filing of the first application.

statement: Tanaka KK enjoys a right of priority for the European patent application for the same invention during a period of twelve months from 3 March 2023, the filing date of its first application.

statement: Where the declaration of priority is not made on filing the European patent application, it may still be made within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: It may still be made within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed.

statement: Where the declaration of priority is not made on filing the European patent application, it may still be made within twelve months from the earliest priority date claimed.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: It may still be made within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed.

scenario: Helios Therapeutics AG files a European patent application on 3 March 2024 claiming priority from an earlier Japanese application filed on 10 January 2023. At the time of filing the European application, Helios's representative omits to include any declaration of priority. The representative later realises the error and wishes to submit the declaration of priority for the first time.
statement: Helios may still validly make the declaration of priority within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed, i.e. measured from 10 January 2023.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: It may still be made within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed.

scenario: Helios Therapeutics AG files a European patent application on 3 March 2024 claiming priority from an earlier Japanese application filed on 10 January 2023. At the time of filing the European application, Helios's representative omits to include any declaration of priority. The representative later realises the error and wishes to submit the declaration of priority for the first time.
statement: Helios may still validly make the declaration of priority within twelve months from the earliest priority date claimed, i.e. measured from 10 January 2023.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: It may still be made within sixteen months from the earliest priority date claimed.

statement: Where the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority for a Euro-PCT application, the applicant must respond to the invitation to comment and amend within a period of six months from the respective communication, failing which the application is deemed to be withdrawn.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the European Patent Office has acted as the International Searching Authority and, where a demand under Article 31 PCT was filed, also as the International Preliminary Examining Authority for a Euro-PCT application, it shall give the applicant the opportunity to comment on the written opinion of the International Searching Authority or the International Preliminary Examination Report and, where appropriate, invite them to correct any deficiencies noted in the written opinion or in the International Preliminary Examination Report and to amend the description, claims and drawings within a period of six months from the respective communication. If the applicant does not comply with or comment on an invitation in accordance with the first or second sentence, the application shall be deemed to be withdrawn.

statement: Where the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority for a Euro-PCT application, the applicant must respond to the invitation to comment and amend within a period of four months from the respective communication, failing which the application is deemed to be withdrawn.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of six months from the respective communication

scenario: Helix Genomics filed an international (PCT) application for which the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority but not as the International Preliminary Examining Authority. On entry into the European phase, the EPO drew up a supplementary European search report. On 3 March 2023, the EPO issued a communication informing Helix Genomics accordingly.
statement: Helix Genomics may amend its application once within six months of the 3 March 2023 communication, and the application as amended will serve as the basis for the supplementary European search.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the application may be amended once within a period of six months from a communication informing the applicant accordingly. The application as amended shall serve as the basis for the supplementary European search.

scenario: Helix Genomics filed an international (PCT) application for which the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority but not as the International Preliminary Examining Authority. On entry into the European phase, the EPO drew up a supplementary European search report. On 3 March 2023, the EPO issued a communication informing Helix Genomics accordingly.
statement: Helix Genomics may amend its application once within four months of the 3 March 2023 communication, and the application as amended will serve as the basis for the supplementary European search.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: the application may be amended once within a period of six months from a communication informing the applicant accordingly. The application as amended shall serve as the basis for the supplementary European search.

topic: Rule 164 – Unity of invention and further searches
statement: Under Rule 164(2)(a) EPC, the Examining Division informs the applicant that a search will be performed in respect of an unsearched invention only if a search fee is paid within a period of two months.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: a search will be performed in respect of any such invention for which a search fee is paid within a period of two months;

topic: Rule 164 – Unity of invention and further searches
statement: Under Rule 164(2)(a) EPC, the Examining Division informs the applicant that a search will be performed in respect of an unsearched invention only if a search fee is paid within a period of four months.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: a search will be performed in respect of any such invention for which a search fee is paid within a period of two months;

topic: Rule 164 – Unity of invention and further searches
scenario: Helix Genomics filed a Euro-PCT application that enters the European phase. The EPO had carried out the international search but, as it dispenses with the supplementary European search report, the Examining Division finds that the application documents claim a second group of inventions that was never searched. The Examining Division decides to inform Helix Genomics that a search will be carried out for any such unsearched invention provided a search fee is paid.
statement: Helix Genomics must pay the search fee for the unsearched invention within a period of two months in order for the search to be performed.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: inform the applicant that a search will be performed in respect of any such invention for which a search fee is paid within a period of two months;

topic: Rule 164 – Unity of invention and further searches
scenario: Helix Genomics filed a Euro-PCT application that enters the European phase. The EPO had carried out the international search but, as it dispenses with the supplementary European search report, the Examining Division finds that the application documents claim a second group of inventions that was never searched. statement: Helix Genomics must pay the search fee for the unsearched invention within a period of four months in order for the search to be performed.
label: FALSE

statement: If the application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based contain more than fifteen claims, claims fees must be paid for the sixteenth claim and every claim after it.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based comprise more than fifteen claims, claims fees shall be paid for the sixteenth and each subsequent claim

statement: If the application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based contain more than ten claims, claims fees must be paid for the eleventh claim and every claim after it.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: If the application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based comprise more than fifteen claims, claims fees shall be paid for the sixteenth and each subsequent claim

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd is entering the European phase of its international application. The application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based comprise 22 claims. Helios's representative reviews the claims-fee obligations under Rule 162 EPC.
statement: Because Helios's application comprises 22 claims, claims fees are due for the sixteenth and each subsequent claim, i.e. for 7 claims.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: claims fees shall be paid for the sixteenth and each subsequent claim

scenario: Helios Optics Ltd is entering the European phase of its international application. The application documents on which the European grant procedure is to be based comprise 22 claims. Helios's representative reviews the claims-fee obligations under Rule 162 EPC.
statement: Because Helios's application comprises 22 claims, claims fees are due for the eleventh and each subsequent claim, i.e. for 12 claims.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: claims fees shall be paid for the sixteenth and each subsequent claim

statement: Where the EPO acted as ISA (and, where a demand was filed, as IPEA) for a Euro-PCT application, the applicant has a period of six months from the relevant communication to comment on the written opinion or IPER and to amend the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of six months from the respective communication

statement: Where the EPO acted as ISA (and, where a demand was filed, as IPEA) for a Euro-PCT application, the applicant has a period of four months from the relevant communication to comment on the written opinion or IPER and to amend the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of six months from the respective communication

scenario: Innova-Tech GmbH filed an international (PCT) application, and the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority. A demand under Article 31 PCT was also filed, and the EPO acted as the International Preliminary Examining Authority. Upon entry into the European phase, the EPO issues a communication under Rule 161(1) EPC inviting Innova-Tech to comment on the negative findings and to correct deficiencies and amend the description, claims and drawings. Innova-Tech neither comments on nor complies with the invitation within the prescribed period.
statement: Innova-Tech has a period of six months from the communication to comment on or comply with the invitation, and because it did neither, its application is deemed to be withdrawn.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: within a period of six months from the respective communication. If the applicant does not comply with or comment on an invitation in accordance with the first or second sentence, the application shall be deemed to be withdrawn.

scenario: Innova-Tech GmbH filed an international (PCT) application, and the EPO acted as the International Searching Authority. A demand under Article 31 PCT was also filed, and the EPO acted as the International Preliminary Examining Authority. Upon entry into the European phase, the EPO issues a communication under Rule 161(1) EPC inviting Innova-Tech to comment on the negative findings and to correct deficiencies and amend the description, claims and drawings. Innova-Tech neither comments on nor complies with the invitation within the prescribed period.
statement: Innova-Tech has a period of four months from the communication to comment on or comply with the invitation, and because it did neither, its application is deemed to be withdrawn.
label: FALSE

statement: Any person may file a notice of opposition to a European patent within nine months of the publication of the mention of the grant in the European Patent Bulletin.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Within nine months of the publication of the mention of the grant of the European patent in the European Patent Bulletin, any person may give notice to the European Patent Office of opposition to that patent

statement: Any person may file a notice of opposition to a European patent within six months of the publication of the mention of the grant in the European Patent Bulletin.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Within nine months of the publication of the mention of the grant of the European patent in the European Patent Bulletin, any person may give notice to the European Patent Office of opposition to that patent

scenario: The mention of the grant of European patent EP 3 456 789, owned by Helvetia Pharma AG, was published in the European Patent Bulletin on 12 March 2025. A competitor, Borealis BioTech Ltd, wishes to challenge the patent and files a notice of opposition with the EPO, but on the filing date it does not pay the opposition fee. Borealis pays the opposition fee three weeks later, still within the period running from the publication of the grant.
statement: Borealis's notice of opposition is not deemed to have been filed until it pays the opposition fee, so the relevant filing date is the date the fee was paid three weeks later.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Notice of opposition shall not be deemed to have been filed until the opposition fee has been paid.

scenario: The mention of the grant of European patent EP 3 456 789, owned by Helvetia Pharma AG, was published in the European Patent Bulletin on 12 March 2025. A competitor, Borealis BioTech Ltd, wishes to challenge the patent and files a notice of opposition with the EPO, but on the filing date it does not pay the opposition fee. Borealis pays the opposition fee three weeks later, still within the period running from the publication of the grant.
statement: Borealis's notice of opposition is deemed to have been filed on the date the written notice was submitted, regardless of the fact that the opposition fee was paid only three weeks later.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Notice of opposition shall not be deemed to have been filed until the opposition fee has been paid.

statement: An opposition against a European patent may be based only on the grounds that its subject-matter is not patentable under Articles 52 to 57, that the patent does not disclose the invention sufficiently clearly and completely for it to be carried out by a skilled person, or that its subject-matter extends beyond the content of the application as filed.
label: TRUE

statement: An opposition against a European patent may be based only on the grounds that its subject-matter is not patentable under Articles 52 to 57, that the patent does not disclose the invention sufficiently clearly and completely for it to be carried out by a skilled person, that its subject-matter extends beyond the content of the application as filed, or that the claims lack clarity.
label: FALSE

scenario: Wavelet GmbH holds European patent EP-X for a wind-turbine blade. A competitor, NordWind AS, wants to file an opposition. NordWind's primary complaint is that the granted claims lack clarity because the term 'substantially aligned' is vague, and the patent's wording is internally inconsistent. NordWind asks its representative whether lack of clarity alone is an admissible ground for opposition.
statement: NordWind cannot base its opposition solely on lack of clarity, because opposition may only be filed on three grounds: lack of patentability under Articles 52 to 57, insufficiency of disclosure, and the subject-matter extending beyond the content of the application as filed.
label: TRUE

scenario: Wavelet GmbH holds European patent EP-X for a wind-turbine blade. A competitor, NordWind AS, wants to file an opposition. NordWind's primary complaint is that the granted claims lack clarity because the term 'substantially aligned' is vague, and the patent's wording is internally inconsistent. NordWind asks its representative whether lack of clarity alone is an admissible ground for opposition.
statement: NordWind cannot base its opposition solely on lack of clarity, because opposition may only be filed on four grounds: lack of patentability under Articles 52 to 57, insufficiency of disclosure, the subject-matter extending beyond the content of the application as filed, and lack of clarity of the claims.
label: FALSE

statement: Grounds for opposition that the opponent did not invoke may nevertheless be examined by the Opposition Division of its own motion where they would prejudice the maintenance of the European patent.
label: TRUE

statement: The Opposition Division may examine the added-matter ground on its own motion, even though Orion did not invoke it, because that ground would prejudice the maintenance of the patent.

statement: A third party who files observations concerning the patentability of an invention under Article 115 EPC does not thereby become a party to the proceedings.
label: TRUE

statement: A third party who files observations concerning the patentability of an invention under Article 115 EPC thereby becomes a party to the proceedings.
label: FALSE

scenario: Helios Optics AG filed a European patent application that was published on 10 January 2023. A competitor, Lumina GmbH, became aware of prior art it believed destroyed the patentability of the claimed invention. In March 2023, while the application was still pending before the European Patent Office, Lumina submitted written observations to the EPO challenging the patentability of Helios's invention, but did not file an opposition or otherwise seek to join the proceedings.
statement: Because Helios's application had already been published, Lumina was permitted to file observations on the patentability of the invention, and by doing so Lumina did not become a party to the proceedings.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: following the publication of the European patent application, any third party may, in accordance with the Implementing Regulations, present observations concerning the patentability of the invention to which the application or patent relates. statement: Where the department whose decision is appealed finds the appeal admissible and well founded, it is required to rectify its decision, but this duty does not apply when the appellant is opposed by another party to the proceedings.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the department whose decision is contested considers the appeal to be admissible and well founded, it shall rectify its decision. This shall not apply where the appellant is opposed by another party to the proceedings.

statement: Where the department whose decision is appealed finds the appeal admissible and well founded, it is required to rectify its decision, and this duty applies even when the appellant is opposed by another party to the proceedings.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: This shall not apply where the appellant is opposed by another party to the proceedings.

scenario: Innovatech GmbH filed a European patent application that was refused by the Examining Division for lack of inventive step. Innovatech, the sole party to the proceedings (there is no opposing party), filed an admissible appeal together with a statement of grounds that the Examining Division receives on 4 March. The Examining Division reviews the appeal and concludes that it is both admissible and well founded.
statement: Because Innovatech is the only party and the Examining Division considers the appeal admissible and well founded, the Examining Division must rectify its decision by way of interlocutory revision.
label: TRUE

scenario: Innovatech GmbH filed a European patent application that was refused by the Examining Division for lack of inventive step. label: FALSE

topic: Rule 99 – Content of the notice of appeal and the statement of grounds
statement: Under Rule 99(1) EPC, the notice of appeal must contain the appellant's name and address, an indication of the decision impugned, and a request defining the subject of the appeal.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span:
(1) The notice of appeal shall contain:
(a) the name and the address of the appellant as provided in Rule 41, paragraph 2(c);
(b) an indication of the decision impugned; and
(c) a request defining the subject of the appeal.

topic: Rule 99 – Content of the notice of appeal and the statement of grounds
statement: Under Rule 99(1) EPC, the notice of appeal must contain the appellant's name and address, an indication of the decision impugned, a request defining the subject of the appeal, and the facts and evidence on which the appeal is based.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span:
(1) The notice of appeal shall contain:
(a) the name and the address of the appellant as provided in Rule 41, paragraph 2(c);
(b) an indication of the decision impugned; and
(c) a request defining the subject of the appeal.
(2) In the statement of grounds of appeal the appellant shall indicate the reasons for setting aside the decision impugned, or the extent to which it is to be amended, and the facts and evidence on which the appeal is based.

topic: Rule 99 – Content of the notice of appeal and the statement of grounds
scenario: Helix Diagnostics GmbH receives an adverse decision from an examining division refusing its European patent application. Within the time limit, it files a notice of appeal that contains an indication of the impugned decision and a request defining the subject of the appeal, but omits the appellant's name and address as required under Rule 41(2)(c). The Board of Appeal notices the missing name and address.
statement: Because the only defect is the missing name and address under Rule 99(1)(a), the Board must communicate this to Helix Diagnostics and invite it to remedy the deficiency within a specified period before any rejection for inadmissibility.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the Board of Appeal notes that the appeal does not comply with Rule 99, paragraph 1(a), it shall communicate this to the appellant and shall invite them to remedy the deficiencies noted within a period to be specified. If the deficiencies are not remedied in due time, the Board of Appeal shall reject the appeal as inadmissible.

topic: Rule 99 – Content of the notice of appeal and the statement of grounds
scenario: Helix Diagnostics GmbH receives an adverse decision from an examining division refusing its European patent application. Within the time limit, it files a notice of appeal that contains an indication of the impugned decision and a request defining the subject of the appeal, but omits the appellant's name and address as required under Rule 41(2)(c). The Board of Appeal notices the missing name and address.
statement: Because the only defect is the missing name and address under Rule 99(1)(a), the Board may immediately reject the appeal as inadmissible without inviting Helix Diagnostics to remedy the deficiency.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: If the Board of Appeal notes that the appeal does not comply with Rule 99, paragraph 1(a), it shall communicate this to the appellant and shall invite them to remedy the deficiencies noted within a period to be specified.

statement: The statement setting out the grounds of appeal must be filed within four months of notification of the decision.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Within four months of notification of the decision, a statement setting out the grounds of appeal shall be filed in accordance with the Implementing Regulations.

statement: The statement setting out the grounds of appeal must be filed within two months of notification of the decision.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Within four months of notification of the decision, a statement setting out the grounds of appeal shall be filed in accordance with the Implementing Regulations.

scenario: Innotech GmbH received notification of the Examining Division's decision to refuse its European patent application on 3 March 2023. Innotech wishes to appeal. Its representative prepares to file the notice of appeal, pay the appeal fee, and later file the statement of grounds.
statement: Innotech must file the statement setting out the grounds of appeal within four months of notification of the decision to refuse its application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Within four months of notification of the decision, a statement setting out the grounds of appeal shall be filed in accordance with the Implementing Regulations.

scenario: Innotech GmbH received notification of the Examining Division's decision to refuse its European patent application on 3 March 2023. Innotech wishes to appeal. Its representative prepares to file the notice of appeal, pay the appeal fee, and later file the statement of grounds.
statement: Innotech must file the statement setting out the grounds of appeal within two months of notification of the decision to refuse its application.
label: FALSE

statement: For the purposes of novelty, the state of the art includes everything made available to the public, whether by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the filing date of the European patent application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The state of the art shall be held to comprise everything made available to the public by means of a written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.

statement: For the purposes of novelty, the state of the art includes everything made available to the public, whether by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the priority date of the European patent application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: The state of the art shall be held to comprise everything made available to the public by means of a written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.

scenario: Helix Biotech filed European patent application EP-A on 10 March 2022. An earlier European patent application EP-B was filed by a third party on 5 January 2022, but EP-B was only published on 12 July 2023. Helix's examiner wishes to know whether the content of EP-B can be cited against the novelty of EP-A.
statement: The content of EP-B as filed forms part of the state of the art for assessing the novelty of EP-A, because EP-B was filed before EP-A's filing date and published after that filing date.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: the content of European patent applications as filed, the dates of filing of which are prior to the date referred to in paragraph 2 and which were published on or after that date, shall be considered as comprised in the state of the art

statement: Under Article 54(2) EPC, the state of the art comprises everything made available to the public by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: The state of the art shall be held to comprise everything made available to the public by means of a written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.

statement: Under Article 54(2) EPC, the state of the art comprises everything made available to the public by written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of publication of the European patent application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: before the date of filing of the European patent application

scenario: Innovatech AG files European patent application EP-A on 15 March 2022, claiming a new polymer composition. An earlier European patent application EP-B was filed by a different company, Polymerix Ltd, on 10 January 2022. EP-B was not published until 20 July 2023 and contains, in its application as filed, an explicit description of the very same polymer composition claimed in EP-A. Innovatech's representative asks whether the content of EP-B can be cited against the novelty of EP-A's claim.
statement: Because EP-B was filed before EP-A's filing date and published afterwards, its content as filed counts as state of the art when assessing the novelty of EP-A's claim.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span:
(1) An invention shall be considered to be new if it does not form part of the state of the art.
(2) The state of the art shall be held to comprise everything made available to the public by means of a written or oral description, by use, or in any other way, before the date of filing of the European patent application.
(3) Additionally, the content of European patent applications as filed, the dates of filing of which are prior to the date referred to in paragraph 2 and which were published on or after that date, shall be considered as comprised in the state of the art.

scenario: Innovatech AG files European patent application EP-A on 15 March 2022, claiming a new polymer composition. label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: (1) An invention shall be considered to be new if it does not form part of the state of the art.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
statement: Where a European patent application is filed in a language that is not an official language of the EPO, the required translation into an official language must be filed within two months of filing the application.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: A translation under Article 14, paragraph 2, shall be filed within two months of filing the European patent application.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
statement: Where a European patent application is filed in a language that is not an official language of the EPO, the required translation into an official language must be filed within one month of filing the application.
label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: A translation under Article 14, paragraph 2, shall be filed within two months of filing the European patent application.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
scenario: Sociedad Innovadora S.L. has its principal place of business in Spain, a Contracting State whose official language (Spanish) is not English, French or German. On 14 March 2023 it files a notice of opposition against a granted European patent, drafting it in Spanish to meet the opposition deadline. The opposition period itself expires on 14 March 2023. Sociedad Innovadora must now supply a translation of the opposition into an official language of the EPO.
statement: Sociedad Innovadora must file the Spanish-to-EPO-language translation of its opposition within one month of filing the document, unless the opposition period expires later, in which case it may file the translation by the end of that period.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: A translation under Article 14, paragraph 4, shall be filed within one month of filing the document. This shall also apply to requests under Article 105a. Where the document is a notice of opposition or appeal, or a statement of grounds of appeal, or a petition for review, the translation may be filed within the period for filing such a notice or statement or petition, if that period expires later.

topic: Article 14 – Languages of the European Patent Office, European patent applications and other documents
scenario: Sociedad Innovadora S.L. has its principal place of business in Spain, a Contracting State whose official language (Spanish) is not English, French or German. On 14 March 2023 it files a notice of opposition against a granted European patent, drafting it in Spanish to meet the opposition deadline. The opposition period itself expires on 14 March 2023. label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: A translation under Article 14, paragraph 4, shall be filed within one month of filing the document.

statement: A party to oral proceedings before the EPO may use an EPO official language other than the language of the proceedings provided it notifies the EPO at least one month before the date of the oral proceedings (or otherwise provides for interpretation into the language of the proceedings).
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Any party to oral proceedings before the European Patent Office may use an official language of the European Patent Office other than the language of the proceedings, if such party gives notice to the European Patent Office at least one month before the date of such oral proceedings or provides for interpretation into the language of the proceedings.

statement: A party to oral proceedings before the EPO may use an EPO official language other than the language of the proceedings provided it notifies the EPO at least two months before the date of the oral proceedings (or otherwise provides for interpretation into the language of the proceedings).

label: TRUE

scenario: The language of the oral proceedings before the EPO in opposition proceedings concerning European patent EP 3 456 789 is English. statement: A party to oral proceedings before the EPO may use an EPO official language other than the language of the proceedings provided it gives notice to the EPO at least one month before the oral proceedings or arranges interpretation into the language of the proceedings.

statement: A party to oral proceedings before the EPO may use an EPO official language other than the language of the proceedings provided it gives notice to the EPO at least two months before the oral proceedings or arranges interpretation into the language of the proceedings.

scenario: Oral proceedings before an Opposition Division are scheduled for 15 May, with the language of the proceedings being English. statement: Helvetia Pharma AG may speak in German at the oral proceedings because it gave notice to the EPO at least one month before the date of the oral proceedings, even though it does not arrange its own interpretation.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Any party to oral proceedings before the European Patent Office may use an official language of the European Patent Office other than the language of the proceedings, if such party gives notice to the European Patent Office at least one month before the date of such oral proceedings or provides for interpretation into the language of the proceedings.

scenario: Oral proceedings before an Opposition Division are scheduled for 15 May, with the language of the proceedings being English. topic: Article 153 – The European Patent Office as designated Office or elected Office

topic: Rule 132 – Periods specified by the European Patent Office
statement: Unless otherwise provided, a period specified by the European Patent Office must be at least two months and no more than four months, though in certain circumstances it may be extended to six months.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Unless otherwise provided, a period specified by the European Patent Office shall be neither less than two months nor more than four months; in certain circumstances it may be up to six months.

topic: Rule 132 – Periods specified by the European Patent Office

label: FALSE
judge_deciding_span: Unless otherwise provided, a period specified by the European Patent Office shall be neither less than two months nor more than four months; in certain circumstances it may be up to six months.

topic: Rule 132 – Periods specified by the European Patent Office
scenario: During examination of a European patent application, the Examining Division issued a communication on 3 March 2023 inviting the applicant Helmuth GmbH to file observations and to specify a period for response. statement: Absent any provision to the contrary, the period the Examining Division specifies for Helmuth GmbH to respond must be neither shorter than two months nor longer than four months.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: Unless otherwise provided, a period specified by the European Patent Office shall be neither less than two months nor more than four months

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights

label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the party concerned considers that the finding of the European Patent Office is inaccurate, it may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights
scenario: NovaTech GmbH filed an international (PCT) application and, on 14 March 2024, received a communication from the EPO under Rule 112(1) noting that a loss of rights had occurred because it had not paid the filing fee within the 31-month period. NovaTech's representative believes the EPO's finding is wrong, as the fee was in fact paid in time.
statement: NovaTech may, within two months of the 14 March 2024 communication, apply for a decision on the matter if it considers the EPO's finding to be inaccurate.
label: TRUE
judge_deciding_span: If the party concerned considers that the finding of the European Patent Office is inaccurate, it may, within two months of the communication under paragraph 1, apply for a decision on the matter.

topic: Rule 112 – Noting of loss of rights

statement: NovaTech may, within four months of the 14 March 2024 communication, apply for a decision on the matter if it considers the EPO's finding to be inaccurate.
